So if you're mailing out a one-ounce sales letter, make sure that that thing weighs almost an ounce. It's wasted real estate if you don't use all the weight you can use. So as I told you, you can get four pages, usually in a one in a letter, under an ounce. Why would you send out anything less than four pages if you can get four pages sent out? On a 4x9 postcard that you mail out, you can also mail out a 4x11 inch postcard for the same price as 4x9 inches. Why would you not use the other two inches? So you might as well print it to 4x11 if you're going to go to 4x9. So always figure out how can I maximize most I can use because the most expensive part of anything in the direct mail is postage. So that's your barometer is when does the postage increase. And so you want to go as much as you can to that desired postage amount. Does that make sense? Yes? Okay.
